ai_strategy_lead.name: Joe Depa
ai_strategy_lead.title: Senior Vice President and Chief Data and AI Officer, Emory University and Emory Healthcare

ai_strategy.summary: Emory University's public AI posture combines published guidance, teaching resources, research centers, AI-relevant programs, and research computing. Public evidence points to distributed governance rather than a verified standalone chief AI officer.

key_provisions: Use appropriate or approved AI tools within institutional data rules
Faculty should set clear course-level AI expectations
Users remain responsible for accuracy, attribution, privacy, and academic integrity
Sensitive data should not be entered into unapproved public tools
ai_policy.summary: Emory combines security guidance for generative AI with AI.Humanity and teaching resources, giving it a broad policy-and-strategy posture around human-centered AI.

bodies.name: AI technology and teaching guidance owners
bodies.scope: Distributed responsibility for AI tool guidance, teaching support, and research coordination.

governance_structure.summary: Emory University's AI governance is distributed across IT, teaching support, and research units.

initiatives.title: Emory University AI guidance and acceptable-use resources
initiatives.description: Emory combines security guidance for generative AI with AI.Humanity and teaching resources, giving it a broad policy-and-strategy posture around human-centered AI.
initiatives.significance: Institution-level guidance is the baseline signal for responsible AI adoption.

investment.description: Policy and guidance work; no dollar amount disclosed.

initiatives.policy_implications: AI use is permitted within privacy, security, course, and attribution guardrails.

initiatives.title: Emory University generative AI teaching resources
initiatives.description: Emory University publishes faculty-facing resources for syllabus language, assignments, assessment, and student expectations around generative AI.
initiatives.significance: Teaching guidance moves AI adoption from conduct enforcement into course design.

investment.description: Faculty support resources; no amount disclosed.

initiatives.policy_implications: Faculty set course-level rules and students need clear disclosure and permitted-use expectations.

initiatives.title: AI.Humanity and Emory AI.Health
initiatives.description: Emory University has AI, machine learning, data science, policy, health, or autonomy research structures that organize faculty and graduate research.
initiatives.significance: Dedicated research structures are a core marker of R1 AI maturity.

investment.description: Research center/institute support; aggregate AI-specific amount not disclosed.

collaborations.description: Cross-school or interdisciplinary AI research ecosystem.

initiatives.title: AI.Humanity curriculum and faculty resources
initiatives.description: Emory University offers AI-relevant degrees, certificates, bootcamps, or structured learning pathways in AI, machine learning, data science, analytics, or computing.
initiatives.significance: AI credentials connect R1 research capacity to student and employer demand.

initiatives.title: Emory University research computing for AI workloads
initiatives.description: Emory provides HPC services for research computing, including data-intensive scientific workloads that support AI research.
initiatives.significance: AI maturity depends on scalable compute as well as policy and curricula.

investment.description: Research computing infrastructure; AI-specific public amount not disclosed.

initiatives.title: Emory University responsible and equitable AI access
initiatives.description: Emory University's public AI resources stress transparent expectations, responsible use, and access-aware teaching or public-interest AI work.
initiatives.significance: Equity evidence distinguishes mature AI adoption from simple tool availability.

investment.description: Access and responsible-use work; no amount disclosed.

initiatives.policy_implications: Clear AI expectations reduce unequal access, ambiguous enforcement, and privacy risk.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in cited source.

tools_and_vendors.name: Microsoft Copilot
tools_and_vendors.role: Secure AI chat service for Emory University and Healthcare.

initiatives.policy_implications: Launch emphasizes credentialed access, confidentiality, and no Microsoft retention of conversational data.
initiatives.title: Microsoft Copilot for Emory community
initiatives.description: Emory launched a secure Microsoft Copilot AI chat service for all members of Emory University and Emory Healthcare, using an Emory-specific instance and emphasizing no-cost access and data-security protections.
initiatives.significance: This turns Emory’s AI platform posture from generic guidance into a named enterprise rollout with named executive sponsorship.

research_centers.name: AI.Humanity and Emory AI.Health
research_centers.focus: AI, machine learning, data science, policy, health, autonomy, or computing research.

research_centers.name: Emory University AI research resource
research_centers.focus: AI, machine learning, data science, policy, health, autonomy, or computing research.

computing_infrastructure.summary: Emory’s HyPER C3 community cloud HPC cluster supports compute-intensive scholarship with AWS-based scientific compute nodes, including GPU-based processors, high-end GPU instances, and high-memory nodes. Shared base-level usage is offered at no cost to active Emory faculty, academic staff, and students through fair-share policies; exact GPU counts are not published.

investments_and_gifts.title: AI.Humanity and Emory AI.Health
investments_and_gifts.purpose: AI research, education, or infrastructure capacity.

industry_and_research_partnerships.purpose: Interdisciplinary AI research and education coordination.

student_facing_ai.name: Course-level AI expectations
student_facing_ai.scope: Students follow instructor rules and institutional guidance for AI use.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Responsible and transparent AI use
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Public guidance emphasizes clarity, responsible use, and access-aware teaching or research practices.

regulatory_context.summary: Emory University operates under federal education, privacy, and research obligations plus campus rules. This sweep found institutional AI guidance but no separate campus-specific AI statute.

narrative_summary: Emory University now has a named AI executive and enterprise platform signal. Joe Depa is publicly identified as senior vice president and chief data and AI officer for Emory University and Emory Healthcare, and Emory launched Microsoft Copilot for all members of both communities in April 2024. The AI strategy remains distributed through AI.Humanity, AI.Health, responsible-governance structures, and health-care AI commitments. Compute evidence is also clearer through HyPER C3, Emory’s AWS-based community cloud HPC cluster with GPU and high-memory resources offered under shared no-cost fair-share access. Exact Copilot usage, contract value, AI budget totals, HyPER C3 GPU counts, and subgroup outcome metrics remain undisclosed.

gaps_acknowledged: President, provost, and CIO names were not independently refreshed in this worker sweep, but the public chief data and AI officer role was added.
Microsoft Copilot is publicly available to Emory University and Healthcare members, but exact contract value, license count, and active usage metrics were not disclosed.
HyPER C3 documents GPU-capable cloud HPC but not a fixed public GPU count; AI-specific total budget and subgroup outcome metrics were not disclosed.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: governance led.
